skill.name: debugging
skill.description: Systematically debug code issues using proven methodologies. Use when encountering errors, unexpected behavior, or performance problems. Handles error analysis, root cause identification, debugging strategies, and fix verification.
skill.summary: Systematically debug code issues using proven debugging methodologies and strategies.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. All 27 static findings are false positives: external_command patterns are educational code examples (git, npm commands); network patterns are legitimate reference URLs; 'weak cryptographic algorithm' triggers are YAML frontmatter delimiters; 'system reconnaissance' triggers are debugging questions. This is a standard debugging methodology skill with no security concerns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Educational Command Examples (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged example commands like `git log` and `npm list` in documentation. These are legitimate educational examples showing debugging techniques, not actual command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Code blocks in documentation showing example commands for teaching debugging - no actual execution or user input injection

dangerous_patterns.title: Reference URLs (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: External links to debugging books and resources (debuggingrules.com, blog.regehr.org, rubberduckdebugging.com). Legitimate educational references.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Static reference URLs in the References section - standard for educational content, no data exfiltration

dangerous_patterns.title: YAML Delimiter False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner misidentified YAML frontmatter `---` as weak cryptographic algorithm. No cryptographic code present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter delimiter is a standard format marker, not cryptographic code

dangerous_patterns.title: Debugging Questions (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged questions like 'When did it start occurring?' as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate debugging methodology questions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Standard debugging questions asking users about their issue context, not system probing

content.value_statement: Struggling with elusive bugs and runtime errors? This skill provides proven debugging methodologies to identify root causes, isolate issues, and implement verified fixes.
actual_capabilities: Analyze error messages and stack traces to identify failure points
Apply systematic debugging methodologies (binary search, divide and conquer)
Reproduce bugs with minimal test cases
Identify common bug patterns (null reference, race conditions, memory leaks)
Implement and verify fixes with regression tests
Use language-specific debuggers and profilers effectively
limitations: Cannot debug issues in environments without access to code or logs
Cannot reproduce environment-specific bugs without environment details
Cannot directly access external systems or databases for live debugging
Intermittent bugs may require additional user context to diagnose

use_cases.title: Fix Runtime Errors
use_cases.description: Analyze TypeError, NullPointerException, or other runtime exceptions to find the root cause and implement a fix.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers encountering crashes or exceptions

use_cases.title: Debug Performance Issues
use_cases.description: Identify memory leaks, slow queries, or performance bottlenecks using profiling techniques.
use_cases.target_user: Developers troubleshooting slow applications or memory issues

use_cases.title: Resolve Hard-to-Reproduce Bugs
use_cases.description: Apply systematic methodologies to track down intermittent failures or race conditions.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers dealing with flaky tests or intermittent failures

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I am getting this error: [paste error message]. Please help me understand what is causing it and how to fix it.
prompt_templates.scenario: User encounters a specific error and needs immediate debugging help

prompt_templates.title: Root Cause Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: My code has been working but started failing recently. Here are the recent changes: [list changes]. The error occurs when: [describe behavior]. Help me identify the root cause.
prompt_templates.scenario: Regression debugging after recent code changes

prompt_templates.title: Performance Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: My application is running slowly. Here are the relevant code sections: [paste code]. Help me identify the performance bottleneck.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Complex Race Condition
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an intermittent failure that only happens in CI. Tests pass locally but fail randomly. Here is the relevant code: [paste code]. Help me identify and fix the race condition.

output_examples.input: TypeError: cannot unpack NoneType object at non-iterable line 25
output_examples.output: Analysis: The error indicates get_user_info() returns None when user not found. Solution: Add return None, None in the not-found case, or raise a custom UserNotFoundError exception.

output_examples.input: Test passes locally but fails intermittently in CI
output_examples.output: This is likely a race condition or shared state issue. Review code for: unsynchronized shared counters, test order dependencies, or async timing issues. Add proper locking or use test isolation techniques.

output_examples.input: Application memory usage grows over time
output_examples.output: Memory leak detected. Common causes: unreleased file handles, growing caches, or event listeners not removed. Use memory_profiler to identify the leak location, then ensure proper cleanup in finally blocks or use context managers.

best_practices: Always reproduce the bug before attempting to fix it
Make one change at a time and verify after each change
Write regression tests to prevent the bug from returning
anti_patterns: Making changes without understanding the root cause
Adding try-catch blocks that silently swallow errors
Ignoring error messages without analyzing them

faq.question: What information should I provide when asking for debugging help?
faq.answer: Provide the full error message, stack trace, the relevant code section, what you expected to happen, and any recent changes to the code.

faq.question: How do I debug a race condition?
faq.answer: Look for shared mutable state accessed by multiple threads. Add proper synchronization using locks, atomic operations, or thread-safe data structures. Consider using thread sanitizers to detect issues.

faq.question: What is the best way to debug memory leaks?
faq.answer: Use memory profiling tools (memory_profiler for Python, Chrome DevTools for JavaScript). Take memory snapshots before and after operations to identify growing objects.

faq.question: How do I debug intermittent failures?
faq.answer: Add detailed logging, check for timing dependencies, look for shared state between tests, and consider running tests in isolation to identify order-dependent issues.

faq.question: Can this skill help with debugging production issues?
faq.answer: Yes, provide the error logs, stack traces, and relevant code. The skill can guide you through systematic debugging even with limited production access.

faq.question: What debugging tools are recommended for different languages?
faq.answer: Python: pdb, ipdb, cProfile. JavaScript: Chrome DevTools. Java: IntelliJ debugger. Go: Delve. Rust: rust-gdb. Each has language-specific strengths.
